skill.name: lead-scraper
skill.description: Create industry lead lists from a target region and industry, with company, contact, phone, and address fields for CSV export.
skill.summary: Build targeted industry lead lists for sales research.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography and entropy findings appear to be false positives in a short markdown skill file. No cryptographic code, obfuscated payload, scripts, or executable behavior was found. The skill purpose involves collecting lead contact data, so publication should require clear privacy, consent, and source-compliance warnings.

medium_findings.title: Lead Contact Data Collection Requires Compliance Review
medium_findings.description: The skill describes collecting company names, contacts, phone numbers, and addresses into CSV output. This can be legitimate B2B research, but it may involve personal data or data-source terms that require consent, lawful basis, and careful handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The stated functionality explicitly includes contact, phone, and address collection. No malicious implementation is present, but the data-handling risk is clear from the skill description.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static alert points to the description line, but that line only describes lead list output fields. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption code, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 contains descriptive front matter text, not executable code. I found no evidence of cryptographic usage in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: The high-entropy alert appears to be caused by front matter and multilingual text in a small markdown file. No binary content, encoded payload, or encrypted block is visible.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file content is readable markdown with Chinese text and metadata. I found no evidence of obfuscation or hidden executable content.

content.value_statement: Sales and research teams often need structured lead lists for a specific region and industry. This skill guides AI-assisted collection and formatting of company, contact, phone, and address details for CSV-ready use.
actual_capabilities: Guides lead list creation from a target region and industry.
Defines output fields for company name, contact, phone, and address.
Frames the workflow for individual users, freelancers, small businesses, and teams.
Reminds users to manually review important AI-generated outputs.
limitations: The skill file does not include scraping scripts or source-specific connectors.
Lead data accuracy depends on the sources and user verification.
Users must respect website terms, privacy laws, and consent requirements.
It does not guarantee complete coverage for any region or industry.

use_cases.title: Build Local Prospect Lists
use_cases.description: Create a CSV-ready list of companies in a city or region for initial sales outreach.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Lead Research
use_cases.description: Collect and organize target accounts for a client campaign before manual validation.

use_cases.title: Map B2B Market Coverage
use_cases.description: Compare companies, contacts, and locations across an industry segment for planning.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Lead List
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one region and industry.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a lead list for [industry] companies in [region]. Include company name, contact, phone, address, and source notes. Keep the output ready for CSV review.

prompt_templates.title: Add Source and Quality Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Add validation fields for manual review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [industry] leads in [region]. For each lead, include company name, contact, phone, address, source, confidence level, and any missing data that needs manual checking.

prompt_templates.title: Segment Leads by Priority
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a prioritized lead list for [industry] in [region]. Group results by likely business fit, location, and available contact details. Explain the priority rules used.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Lead List for Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this lead list for duplicate entries, missing fields, source quality, and privacy concerns. Flag entries that need consent checks or source verification before outreach.

output_examples.input: Find packaging suppliers in Austin for a small manufacturing outreach campaign.
output_examples.output: A structured lead list with company names, available contact details, addresses, source notes, and manual review flags.

output_examples.input: Prepare restaurant equipment leads in Toronto and mark incomplete records.
output_examples.output: A CSV-ready prospect list grouped by data completeness, with missing phone numbers and uncertain contacts clearly marked.

output_examples.input: Review a draft lead list before a sales campaign.
output_examples.output: A cleaned list summary with duplicates removed, risky entries flagged, and recommended fields for manual verification.

best_practices: Use public, permitted, and reputable sources for lead research.
Verify phone numbers, contacts, and addresses before outreach.
Document each source so records can be reviewed or removed later.
anti_patterns: Do not collect private personal data without a lawful basis.
Do not ignore website terms, robots rules, or consent requirements.
Do not send outreach from unverified or stale contact records.

faq.question: Does this skill include a working scraper?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file provides a workflow description, not executable scraping code.

faq.question: What inputs does the skill expect?
faq.answer: It is designed around a target region and industry.

faq.question: What output fields are expected?
faq.answer: The description lists company name, contact, phone, and address fields for CSV output.

faq.question: Can I use it for regulated or personal data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Confirm consent, lawful basis, and source permissions before collecting or using personal data.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Should generated leads be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill notes that important AI outputs should be manually reviewed.
